# Ideals generated by elements of semirings

```agda
module ring-theory.ideals-generated-by-elements-semirings where
```

open import foundation.dependent-pair-types

## Idea

An {{#concept "ideal generated by an element" Disambiguation="semiring" Agda=ideal-element-Semiring}} `x` of a [semiring](ring-theory.semirings.md) `R` is the [ideal generated by the subset](ring-theory.ideals-generated-by-subsets-semirings.lagda.md) containing only the element `x`. In other words, the ideal generated by `x` is the least [ideal](ring-theory.ideals-semirings.md) that contains the element `x`. In informal writing, the ideal generated by the element `x` is denoted by `⟨x⟩`.

## Definitions

### The predicate of being an ideal generated by an element

```agda
module _
  {l1 l2 : Level} (R : Semiring l1) (x : type-Semiring R)
  (J : ideal-Semiring l2 R)
  where

  is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring : UUω
  is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring =
    {l3 : Level} (K : ideal-Semiring l3 R) →
    leq-ideal-Semiring R J K ↔ is-in-ideal-Semiring R K x

  contains-element-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring :
    is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring → is-in-ideal-Semiring R J x
  contains-element-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring U =
    forward-implication (U J) (refl-leq-ideal-Semiring R J)

  leq-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring :
    is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring →
    {l3 : Level} (K : ideal-Semiring l3 R) (k : is-in-ideal-Semiring R K x) →
    leq-ideal-Semiring R J K
  leq-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring U K =
    backward-implication (U K)
```

### The ideal generated by an element of a semiring

```agda
module _
  {l1 : Level} (R : Semiring l1) (x : type-Semiring R)
  where

  generating-subset-ideal-element-Semiring :
    subset-Semiring l1 R
  generating-subset-ideal-element-Semiring =
    subtype-im x

  ideal-element-Semiring :
    ideal-Semiring l1 R
  ideal-element-Semiring =
    ideal-subset-Semiring R generating-subset-ideal-element-Semiring

  subset-ideal-element-Semiring :
    subset-Semiring l1 R
  subset-ideal-element-Semiring =
    subset-ideal-subset-Semiring R
      generating-subset-ideal-element-Semiring

  is-in-ideal-element-Semiring :
    type-Semiring R → UU l1
  is-in-ideal-element-Semiring =
    is-in-ideal-subset-Semiring R
      generating-subset-ideal-element-Semiring

  is-prop-is-in-ideal-element-Semiring :
    (r : type-Semiring R) → is-prop (is-in-ideal-element-Semiring r)
  is-prop-is-in-ideal-element-Semiring =
    is-prop-is-in-ideal-Semiring R ideal-element-Semiring

  type-ideal-element-Semiring : UU l1
  type-ideal-element-Semiring =
    type-subset-Semiring R subset-ideal-element-Semiring

  inclusion-ideal-element-Semiring :
    type-ideal-element-Semiring → type-Semiring R
  inclusion-ideal-element-Semiring =
    inclusion-subset-Semiring R subset-ideal-element-Semiring

  ap-inclusion-ideal-element-Semiring :
    (y z : type-ideal-element-Semiring) → y ＝ z →
    inclusion-ideal-element-Semiring y ＝
    inclusion-ideal-element-Semiring z
  ap-inclusion-ideal-element-Semiring =
    ap-inclusion-subset-Semiring R subset-ideal-element-Semiring

  is-in-subset-inclusion-ideal-element-Semiring :
    (y : type-ideal-element-Semiring) →
    is-in-ideal-element-Semiring
      ( inclusion-ideal-element-Semiring y)
  is-in-subset-inclusion-ideal-element-Semiring =
    is-in-subset-inclusion-subset-Semiring R
      subset-ideal-element-Semiring

  is-closed-under-eq-ideal-element-Semiring :
    {y z : type-Semiring R} →
    is-in-ideal-element-Semiring y →
    (y ＝ z) → is-in-ideal-element-Semiring z
  is-closed-under-eq-ideal-element-Semiring =
    is-closed-under-eq-subset-Semiring R
      subset-ideal-element-Semiring

  is-closed-under-eq-ideal-element-Semiring' :
    {y z : type-Semiring R} →
    is-in-ideal-element-Semiring z →
    (y ＝ z) → is-in-ideal-element-Semiring y
  is-closed-under-eq-ideal-element-Semiring' =
    is-closed-under-eq-subset-Semiring' R
      subset-ideal-element-Semiring

  is-ideal-subset-ideal-element-Semiring :
    is-ideal-subset-Semiring R subset-ideal-element-Semiring
  is-ideal-subset-ideal-element-Semiring =
    is-ideal-ideal-Semiring R ideal-element-Semiring

  is-additive-submonoid-ideal-element-Semiring :
    is-additive-submonoid-subset-Semiring R
      subset-ideal-element-Semiring
  is-additive-submonoid-ideal-element-Semiring =
    is-additive-submonoid-ideal-Semiring R ideal-element-Semiring

  contains-zero-ideal-element-Semiring :
    is-in-ideal-element-Semiring (zero-Semiring R)
  contains-zero-ideal-element-Semiring =
    contains-zero-ideal-Semiring R ideal-element-Semiring

  is-closed-under-addition-ideal-element-Semiring :
    is-closed-under-addition-subset-Semiring R
      subset-ideal-element-Semiring
  is-closed-under-addition-ideal-element-Semiring =
    is-closed-under-addition-ideal-Semiring R ideal-element-Semiring

  is-closed-under-left-multiplication-ideal-element-Semiring :
    is-closed-under-left-multiplication-subset-Semiring R
      subset-ideal-element-Semiring
  is-closed-under-left-multiplication-ideal-element-Semiring =
    is-closed-under-left-multiplication-ideal-Semiring R
      ideal-element-Semiring

  is-closed-under-right-multiplication-ideal-element-Semiring :
    is-closed-under-right-multiplication-subset-Semiring R
      subset-ideal-element-Semiring
  is-closed-under-right-multiplication-ideal-element-Semiring =
    is-closed-under-right-multiplication-ideal-Semiring R
      ideal-element-Semiring

  is-closed-under-two-sided-multiplication-ideal-element-Semiring :
    is-closed-under-two-sided-multiplication-subset-Semiring R
      subset-ideal-element-Semiring
  is-closed-under-two-sided-multiplication-ideal-element-Semiring =
    is-closed-under-two-sided-multiplication-ideal-Semiring R
      ideal-element-Semiring

  additive-submonoid-ideal-element-Semiring :
    Submonoid l1 (additive-monoid-Semiring R)
  additive-submonoid-ideal-element-Semiring =
    additive-submonoid-ideal-Semiring R ideal-element-Semiring

  contains-element-ideal-element-Semiring :
    is-in-ideal-element-Semiring x
  contains-element-ideal-element-Semiring i =
    contains-subset-ideal-subset-Semiring R
      ( generating-subset-ideal-element-Semiring)
      ( x i)
      ( unit-trunc-Prop (i , refl))

  abstract
    is-ideal-generated-by-element-ideal-element-Semiring :
      is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring R x
        ideal-element-Semiring
    is-ideal-generated-by-element-ideal-element-Semiring
      J =
      is-image-subtype-subtype-im x (subset-ideal-Semiring R J) ∘iff
      is-ideal-generated-by-subset-ideal-subset-Semiring R
        ( generating-subset-ideal-element-Semiring)
        ( J)
```

## Properties

### Any two ideals generated by the same element contain the same elements

```agda
module _
  {l1 l2 l3 l4 : Level} (R : Semiring l1) {I : UU l2} (x : I → type-Semiring R)
  (J : ideal-Semiring l3 R)
  (U : is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring R x J)
  (K : ideal-Semiring l4 R)
  (V : is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring R x K)
  where

  has-same-elements-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring :
    has-same-elements-ideal-Semiring R J K
  pr1 (has-same-elements-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring r) =
    leq-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring R x J U K
      ( contains-element-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring
        R x K V)
      ( r)
  pr2 (has-same-elements-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring r) =
    leq-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring R x K V J
      ( contains-element-is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring
        R x J U)
      ( r)
```

### Any ideal that has the same elements as the ideal generated by an element `x` satisfies the universal property of the ideal generated by the element `x`

```agda
module _
  {l1 l2 l3 : Level} (R : Semiring l1) {I : UU l2} (x : I → type-Semiring R)
  (J : ideal-Semiring l3 R)
  where

  is-ideal-generated-by-element-has-same-elements-Semiring :
    has-same-elements-ideal-Semiring R
      ( ideal-element-Semiring R x)
      ( J) →
    is-ideal-generated-by-element-Semiring R x J
  is-ideal-generated-by-element-has-same-elements-Semiring H K =
    logical-equivalence-reasoning
      leq-ideal-Semiring R J K
      ↔ leq-ideal-Semiring R (ideal-element-Semiring R x) K
        by
        iff-Π-iff-family
          ( λ y →
            ( λ f → f ∘ forward-implication (H y)) ,
            ( λ f → f ∘ backward-implication (H y)))
      ↔ ((i : I) → is-in-ideal-Semiring R K (x i))
        by
        is-ideal-generated-by-element-ideal-element-Semiring
          R x K
```

### The image of the ideal `⟨x⟩` generated by an element `x` under a semiring homomorphism `f` is the ideal `⟨f ∘ x⟩` generated by the element `f x`

There are three ways to state this fact:

1. The image of the ideal `⟨x⟩` under the semiring homomorphism `f` has the same
   elements as the ideal `⟨f ∘ x⟩`.
2. The ideal `⟨f ∘ x⟩` satisfies the universal property of the image of the
   ideal `⟨x⟩` under the semiring homomorphism `f`.
3. The image of the ideal `⟨x⟩` under the semiring homomorphism `f` satisfies
   the universal property of the ideal generated by the element
   `f ∘ x`.

```agda
module _
  {l1 l2 l3 : Level} (R : Semiring l1) (S : Semiring l2) (f : hom-Semiring R S)
  {I : UU l3} (x : I → type-Semiring R)
  where

  compute-image-ideal-element-Semiring :
    has-same-elements-ideal-Semiring S
      ( im-Semiring R S f (ideal-element-Semiring R x))
      ( ideal-element-Semiring S (map-hom-Semiring R S f ∘ x))
  compute-image-ideal-element-Semiring = ?
```

```text
module _
  {l1 l2 l3 : Level} (G : Group l1) (H : Group l2) (f : hom-Group G H)
  {I : UU l3} (x : I → type-Group G)
  where

  compute-image-subgroup-element-Group :
    has-same-elements-Subgroup H
      ( im-hom-Subgroup G H f (subgroup-element-Group G x))
      ( subgroup-element-Group H (map-hom-Group G H f ∘ x))
  compute-image-subgroup-element-Group h =
    logical-equivalence-reasoning
      is-in-im-hom-Subgroup G H f (subgroup-element-Group G x) h
      ↔ is-in-subgroup-subset-Group H
          ( im-subtype (map-hom-Group G H f) (subtype-im x))
          ( h)
        by
        compute-image-subgroup-subset-Group G H f (subtype-im x) h
      ↔ is-in-subgroup-element-Group H (map-hom-Group G H f ∘ x) h
        by
        inv-iff
          ( has-same-elements-subgroup-subset-has-same-elements-subset-Group H
            ( subtype-im (map-hom-Group G H f ∘ x))
            ( im-subtype (map-hom-Group G H f) (subtype-im x))
            ( compute-subtype-im-comp (map-hom-Group G H f) x)
            ( h))

  is-image-subgroup-element-Group :
    is-image-subgroup-hom-Group G H f
      ( subgroup-element-Group G x)
      ( subgroup-element-Group H (map-hom-Group G H f ∘ x))
  is-image-subgroup-element-Group =
    is-image-subgroup-has-same-elements-Subgroup G H f
      ( subgroup-element-Group G x)
      ( subgroup-element-Group H (map-hom-Group G H f ∘ x))
      ( compute-image-subgroup-element-Group)

  is-subgroup-generated-by-element-image-subgroup-element-Group :
    is-subgroup-generated-by-element-Group H
      ( map-hom-Group G H f ∘ x)
      ( im-hom-Subgroup G H f (subgroup-element-Group G x))
  is-subgroup-generated-by-element-image-subgroup-element-Group =
    is-subgroup-generated-by-element-has-same-elements-Subgroup H
      ( map-hom-Group G H f ∘ x)
      ( im-hom-Subgroup G H f (subgroup-element-Group G x))
      ( inv-iff ∘ compute-image-subgroup-element-Group)
```

### The subgroup `⟨xᵢ⟩` is trivial if and only if `unit-Group G ＝ xᵢ` for every `i`

```text
module _
  {l1 l2 : Level} (G : Group l1) {I : UU l2} (x : I → type-Group G)
  where

  is-trivial-subgroup-element-Group :
    ((i : I) → is-unit-Group' G (x i)) →
    is-trivial-Subgroup G (subgroup-element-Group G x)
  is-trivial-subgroup-element-Group =
    leq-subgroup-element-Group G x (trivial-Subgroup G)

  is-family-of-units-is-trivial-subgroup-element-Group :
    is-trivial-Subgroup G (subgroup-element-Group G x) →
    (i : I) → is-unit-Group' G (x i)
  is-family-of-units-is-trivial-subgroup-element-Group H i =
    H (x i) (contains-element-subgroup-element-Group G x i)
```
